jd_text:
Job PurposeThe Subsurface Director leads all subsurface activities of the company. This includes geology, geophysics, reservoir engineering, petrophysics, geomechanics, and subsurface data. The role covers the full lifecycle—from exploration to field development and production—and ensures safe, efficient, and maximum recovery of resources while supporting the company’s long-term energy strategy.
Experience & Qualifications10+ years of subsurface experience in the energy sector (oil & gas, geothermal, CCS, hydrogen storage, or integrated energy).Strong experience in exploration, appraisal, field development, and reservoir management.Proven track record in reserves growth and production optimisation.Experience leading large technical teams.Proficient in industry software (Petrel, Eclipse/CMG, DecisionSpace, Geolog, OFM, etc.).Master’s degree in a relevant technical field (or equivalent professional certifications).
Key Responsibilities1. Strategy & Technical LeadershipDevelop and implement the company’s subsurface strategy.Ensure high-quality technical evaluations of prospects, reservoirs, and field development plans.Set and control subsurface technical standards and workflows.2. Resource & Reserves ManagementLead resources from early concepts through to reserves.Oversee reserves estimation and classification (PRMS or equivalent).Approve reserves booking, production forecasts, and depletion plans.3. Geology & GeophysicsOversee seismic acquisition, processing, and interpretation.Ensure accurate geological and reservoir models.Support exploration, appraisal, and development drilling decisions.4. Reservoir Engineering & Field DevelopmentLead reservoir studies, simulation, and production forecasting.Approve Field Development Plans (FDPs), well locations, and recovery strategies.Ensure continuous monitoring and optimization of reservoirs.5. Production OptimizationWork closely with Operations and Drilling teams to improve production.Support problem-solving for underperforming wells.Ensure long-term reservoir health and integrity.6. Technology & Data ManagementPromote use of digital tools, automation, and AI/ML technologies.Ensure proper management and quality of subsurface data.Encourage innovation to improve efficiency and accuracy.7. Collaboration & Stakeholder ManagementWork with drilling, operations, HSE, commercial, and project teams.Liaise with joint venture partners, regulators, and consultants.Provide subsurface input for business development, M&A, and due diligence.8. Leadership & Team DevelopmentLead and develop a multidisciplinary subsurface team.Build team capability through coaching and succession planning.Promote a strong culture of safety, collaboration, and technical excellence.Key Skills & CompetenciesStrong strategic and analytical thinking.Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.Ability to make decisions under uncertainty.Proven leadership in multicultural and multidisciplinary environments.High integrity and safety-focused mindset.Strong problem-solving abilities.
